questions.q: In which Indian state is Gwalior Fort located?
questions.explanation: Gwalior Fort sits above the city of Gwalior in the central Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: On what kind of terrain is Gwalior Fort built?
questions.explanation: The fort crowns a steep, isolated sandstone hill (Gopachal) that rises sharply above the surrounding plain.

questions.q: By what nickname is Gwalior Fort often known?
questions.explanation: Because of its formidable, nearly impregnable hilltop position, Gwalior Fort has been called 'the Gibraltar of India'.

questions.q: Which famous palace inside Gwalior Fort is decorated with blue tiles?
questions.explanation: The Man Mandir palace, built by Raja Man Singh Tomar, is renowned for its bright blue glazed-tile decoration.

questions.q: Gwalior Fort is best described as which type of structure?
questions.explanation: Gwalior Fort is a large hilltop fortress containing palaces, temples and water tanks within its walls.

questions.q: Which Hindu king of the Tomar dynasty built the Man Mandir palace at Gwalior?
questions.explanation: Raja Man Singh Tomar of the Tomar dynasty built the celebrated Man Mandir palace in the late 15th to early 16th century.

questions.q: What is the dominant building material of Gwalior Fort's walls and palaces?
questions.explanation: The fort and its palaces are built largely of sandstone quarried from the very hill on which it stands.

questions.q: Inside Gwalior Fort, the Sas Bahu temples are dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The 11th-century Sas Bahu (Sahastrabahu) temples within the fort are dedicated to Vishnu.

questions.q: Which royal family ruled Gwalior in the 18th to 20th centuries and is closely linked with the fort?
questions.explanation: The Scindia (Sindhia) Maratha rulers governed Gwalior state and held the fort until the modern era.

questions.q: What feature gives the Man Mandir palace its distinctive colourful appearance?
questions.explanation: Glazed tiles in blue, green and yellow, including motifs of ducks and elephants, decorate the Man Mandir's facade.

questions.q: Which dynasty did Raja Man Singh, builder of the Man Mandir, belong to?
questions.explanation: Raja Man Singh, who built the Man Mandir palace, was a ruler of the Tomar (Tomara) dynasty of Gwalior.

questions.q: The towering Teli Ka Mandir inside Gwalior Fort is notable for what architectural feature?
questions.explanation: The Teli Ka Mandir is famous for its tall, barrel-vaulted (wagon-roof) shikhara blending North and South Indian styles.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor seized Gwalior Fort and used it as a state prison?
questions.explanation: Gwalior Fort came under Mughal control and was used as a prison; Aurangzeb notably had prisoners, including his brother Murad, held and executed there.

questions.q: The earliest known inscription mentioning the numeral 'zero' as a written symbol is found in a temple inside which fort?
questions.explanation: A 9th-century inscription in the Chaturbhuj temple at Gwalior Fort contains one of the oldest known written uses of zero as a symbol.

questions.q: Which Sikh Guru was imprisoned at Gwalior Fort and later released, an event commemorated by Sikhs?
questions.explanation: Guru Hargobind was held at Gwalior Fort by Jahangir; his release is commemorated by Sikhs, linked with Bandi Chhor Divas.

questions.q: A gurudwara built inside Gwalior Fort honours which Sikh Guru's captivity there?
questions.explanation: The Gurudwara Data Bandi Chhor inside the fort marks the place where Guru Hargobind was imprisoned and freed.

questions.q: Which 1857 rebellion leader fought her final battle near Gwalior and died in the area?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi captured Gwalior during the 1857 uprising and was killed fighting British forces near the fort.

questions.q: The Sas Bahu temples in Gwalior Fort were built during which period?
questions.explanation: The Sas Bahu (Sahastrabahu) temples date to roughly the 11th century CE, built under the Kachchhapaghata rulers.

questions.q: What are the large rock-cut figures carved into the cliffs around Gwalior Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort hill is famous for colossal rock-cut Jain Tirthankara statues carved into the cliff faces (the Gopachal and Siddhachal groups).

questions.q: Which legendary king is traditionally credited with founding Gwalior and giving it its name?
questions.explanation: Local legend credits King Suraj Sen, cured by the sage Gwalipa, with founding the fort and naming the city Gwalior.

questions.q: The Gujari Mahal within Gwalior Fort was built by Raja Man Singh for his queen and now serves as what?
questions.explanation: The Gujari Mahal, built for Queen Mrignayani, today houses an archaeological museum displaying sculptures and antiquities.

questions.q: Approximately how tall do the sandstone walls and ramparts of Gwalior Fort rise above the plain at the hill's edge?
questions.explanation: The fort sits atop sheer cliffs, with walls and the rock face rising tens of metres (roughly 35 m and more) above the plain.

questions.q: For which queen did Raja Man Singh Tomar build the Gujari Mahal?
questions.explanation: Raja Man Singh Tomar (reigned 1486-1516) built the Gujari Mahal for his beloved Gujar queen, Mrignayani.

questions.q: The famous inscription with one of the earliest recorded zeros at Gwalior Fort dates to roughly which year?
questions.explanation: The Chaturbhuj temple inscription, recording a garden's dimensions with the symbol for zero, is dated to about 876 CE.

questions.q: Which dynasty is generally credited with constructing the Sas Bahu temples at Gwalior?
questions.explanation: The Sas Bahu temples were built in the 11th century under the Kachchhapaghata rulers of the Gwalior region.

questions.q: Mughal emperor Babur, in his memoirs, objected to and ordered the partial defacing of which features at Gwalior?
options: The blue tiles of Man Mandir
questions.explanation: Babur recorded the great naked Jain rock idols at Gwalior in the Baburnama, objected to them, and ordered some of them defaced.

questions.q: The Teli Ka Mandir at Gwalior is dedicated primarily to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Teli Ka Mandir, the tallest structure in the fort, is generally regarded as a temple dedicated to Vishnu.

questions.q: Roughly how tall is the Teli Ka Mandir, the tallest building inside Gwalior Fort?
questions.explanation: The Teli Ka Mandir rises to about 30 metres (roughly 100 feet), making it the tallest structure in the fort.

questions.q: After capturing Gwalior in 1857, the rebel leaders proclaimed which Maratha figure as their Peshwa?
questions.explanation: When the 1857 rebels took Gwalior, they declared Nana Sahib as Peshwa before the British recaptured the fort.

questions.q: The 16th-century musician Tansen, one of the 'navaratnas' of Akbar's court, is associated with which city tied to Gwalior Fort?
questions.explanation: The legendary musician Tansen hailed from Gwalior, whose musical tradition flourished under the Tomar kings of the fort, and his tomb stands in the city.
